description: Evidence classification taxonomy for FinOps audit. Defines what constitutes valid evidence for each score level.

0.label: Absent
0.description: No evidence found in the document. Document is silent on this topic.
0.evidence_requirements: None required. Silence is valid data.

1.label: Aspirational
1.description: Vague intent, buzzwords, or planned initiatives only. No evidence of operational practice.
1.maturity_interpretation: Intent without implementation. Plans = Score 1 max.
1.antipattern_interpretation: Minor indicators or emerging risk signals.
evidence_examples: We plan to implement cost tagging
Cloud cost optimization is on our roadmap
We are considering a FinOps team
Cost awareness is important to us

2.label: Operational
2.description: Behavior or process is described and functioning. Evidence of actual practice, not just policy.
2.maturity_interpretation: Capability exists and is practiced. Not yet mature.
2.antipattern_interpretation: Pattern is present and causing friction.
evidence_examples: We review cloud costs monthly with engineering leads
Tags are applied to most production resources
Budget alerts are configured for major accounts

3.label: Embedded
3.description: Explicit mechanisms, enforcement, automation, or cultural norms. Evidence of mature, systematic practice.
3.maturity_interpretation: Mature capability with enforcement and measurement.
3.antipattern_interpretation: Pattern is deeply embedded and structural.
evidence_examples: Automated policy blocks untagged resource deployment
Cost-per-transaction is tracked in real-time dashboards
FinOps CoE runs quarterly optimization campaigns with tracked savings
Architecture reviews require cost modeling before approval

evidence_quality_rules: Direct quotes from the document are the strongest evidence
Paraphrased summaries of described behaviors are acceptable
References to tools, processes, or metrics are evidence of practice
Job titles or team names that imply FinOps function are evidence
Future tense ('we will', 'we plan to') caps score at 1
Past tense ('we implemented', 'we migrated') indicates operational evidence
Specific numbers, percentages, or metrics strengthen evidence quality
Generic industry jargon without context is weak evidence (Score 1)

evidence_categories.description: Orthogonal to score. Every evidence_quote captured in Phase 1 must be tagged with exactly one of these seven categories.

categories.Policy: Written rules, standards, or formal documents that DECLARE intent. Examples: tagging policy, budget approval policy, cost governance charter.
categories.Process: Recurring human practices or workflows that are described as actually happening. Examples: monthly cost review meetings, quarterly architecture reviews, weekly tag audits.
categories.Operational: Day-to-day tactical activities and roles. Examples: a FinOps analyst rightsizes EC2 weekly, a team triages anomaly alerts daily.
categories.Automation: Code, scripts, or platform features that ENFORCE without human intervention. Examples: CI/CD blocks untagged resources, Lambda kills idle instances, IaC policy-as-code.
categories.Accountability: Mechanisms that assign ownership and consequences. Examples: cost-center showback, team-level chargeback, cost is a KPI on engineering scorecards.
categories.Financial-Integration: Cost data is wired into financial systems or business decisions. Examples: cloud spend reconciled with GL, unit-cost-per-transaction reported to product, ROI gates investment.
categories.Cultural: Beliefs, norms, and incentives that shape behavior. Examples: engineers cite cost in design docs, cost is mentioned in standups, savings are celebrated.

tagging_rules: Every evidence_quote MUST carry exactly one category from the seven above.
If a quote could plausibly fit multiple categories, pick the dominant one (the one the quote most directly evidences).
Automation supersedes Policy when the quote describes enforcement, not just declaration.
Cultural supersedes Process when the quote describes a norm or belief, not a scheduled activity.

false_positive_guards: Mentioning a tool name does not mean the tool is actively used
Having a dashboard does not mean anyone looks at it
Budget existence does not mean budget enforcement
A FinOps job title does not mean FinOps practice
Savings reported does not mean savings verified
Policy documented does not mean policy enforced
